props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · Variational Helium — Screened Nuclear Charge

beats.narration_text: Ciao. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of variational helium. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of variational helium

beats.narration_text: Here is why Variational Helium — Screened Nuclear Charge belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.
Variational Helium is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. He atom (Z = 2) — trial wavefunction ψ ∝ e. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Variational Helium. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: zstar-slider. Verify: He atom (Z = 2) — trial wavefunction ψ ∝ e. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Variational Helium. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: zstar-slider. Verify: He atom (Z = 2) — trial wavefu

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from qmcg-variational-helium.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: variational helium. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 0.5 to 3.0. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 0.5 to 3.0

beats.narration_text: He atom (Z = 2) — trial wavefunction ψ ∝ e. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of variational helium. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Variational Helium simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Variational Helium simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
